item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved the "Rick and North" and the wonderful felling that this article bring for me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the extended log has an incredible amount of potential, I should add to it some time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Extended Log of SCP-2444 Vendors is now live and open to new submissions! Feel free to add your own vendors.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At this point, I feel like the trope of "anomalous bootleg" is pretty played out. It was a bit of a change up to see it played for feels rather than the standard "lol anomalous thing doesn't understand persons" bit, but it was only at the end that there was any indication I was supposed to find it vaguely sad rather than vaguely funny.

This article doesn't really do much to change up the idea, and by the end I was pretty much just skimming. -1

item.title: Vendor ideas
item.content:encoded:
Roget told me he'd consider making a collaborative log of vendors if we submitted enough ideas in the comments. I've put my ideas under the collapsible below

clicc do instantly die of aspartylglucosaminuria
sorry no aspartylglucosaminuria for u

Name Given:
Description:
Common items sold:

Name Given: Karen
Description: A female California condor (Gymnogyps californianu). Communicates using a portable text-to-speech device that it operates using its toes. Both wings appear to have suffered severe bone fractures in the past which have healed improperly, greatly limiting its flight capability.
Common items sold:

  • Unpackaged strips of beef jerky
  • A large piece of dried wood, inscribed with unknown writing. Despite claiming that the item is for sale, the vendor ignores all attempts to purchase it or further questions about the item or its price. Attempts to touch the object causes the vendor to become severely agitated.
  • Small wooden crosses, each with a roughly cross-shaped mass of living muscle tissue stapled to it. Genetic testing indicates that the tissue is sourced from various coyotes ((Canis latrans))
  • Empty sheets of A4 paper. Sheets are frequently stained with dirt, ink, dust, or engine oil.

Name Given: None
Description: Female human of Inuit descent, aged approximately 7-8. Has an extraneous arm beginning from the left elbow, covered in burn scars appearing chemical in nature. Frequently indicates that her position as a vendor is temporary and that she is waiting for an individual in a position of authority over her to replace her, and that this individual has forbidden her from stating her name or the name of this individual. Fluent in Inuktitut, French, Russian, and English.
Common items sold:

  • Postcards depicting various locations in an unknown city. Images most commonly depict decrepit buildings from a large variety of cultures and architectural styles being consumed by living tissue, as well as tall spires and ziggurats consisting of similar tissue underneath rose-colored skies. All postcards are labeled in a as-of-yet untranslated language
  • Large bags of dog food, most of which either match no known brand or match brands which are discontinued
  • Live moon jellyfish (Aurelia aurita) stored in a large water-filled glass jar. Vendor will occasionally remove a jellyfish from the jar and inspect it, before either returning it to the jar, throwing it onto the ground and stomping on it, or consuming it.
  • Various unlabeled pills, a vast majority of which are either inert or contain varying dosages of various types of androgenic-anabolic steroids, both known and unknown.

Name Given: Caesar, Calvin, Callum
Description: An automaton constructed out of various often decrepit computer parts assembled around a humanoid frame.
Common items sold:

  • Jewelry poorly modified to include imagery associated with the Steampunk subculture or the Church of the Broken God.
  • Heavily damaged screwdrivers, some unusable
  • Various instruction manuals for electronic devices, most of which are unreadable due to large amounts of apparent translation errors, and most of which contain subtle memetic agents meant to induce admiration for the electronics industry. The functions of most of the devices depicted in these manuals is unknown.
  • Instances of SCP-2534 sold as collector's items

Name Given: Mr. Jeffers
Description: An animate, humanoid mass of lint wearing a ski suit, gloves, a knitted beanie, and large black sunglasses. Claims that "Mr. Jeffers" is a pseudonym, but refuses to elaborate on its reason not to reveal its full name.
Common items sold:

  • Various items of clothing, often disproportionately sized for humans.
  • Table fans.

Name Given: Charlotte
Description: Caucasian human suffering from generalized hypertrichosis. Speaks with a british accent
Common items sold:

  • Glass bottles containing various types of juice and soda, with most branding indicating products found nowhere in baseline reality. All bottles have been opened, emptied, refilled with a different liquid than the branding would indicate, and then resealed before sale. Most are contaminated with various bacteria.
  • Various severely rusted metal objects.
  • Various potted houseplants, most matching no known terrestrial species. All are in poor health

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
made a couple really small grammatical edits i saw as i read.

i like extradimensional flea market. i like it a lot. easy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn’t want to overdo it, I tried to just use my best ideas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the variety of the vendors descriptions, as well as their wares, really sells the article and makes it feel especially dream-like. I wouldn't mind seeing an extended list of items, it might be a good addition. Either way, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Most frequently, these dreams take the form of the subject owning physical possessions, oftentimes becoming physically conjoined with these objects and occasionally becoming a piece of merchandise themselves.

Speaking as someone who is a broken record of "I hate dream skips", this is fucked up and I love it.

snipping off

Grammar's off here, but it'd probably be better as "removed" anyway.

Needs spaces before the ('s in the Mr. Messy section.

Overall, this gets a little goofy by the end, but it reminds me of other good "stuff from another dimension" skips, like, I dunno, the nuclear hot dog cart. I mean, clothing with no holes, that's inspired.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I might be able to come up with something if the people will it…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first I thought this was weird-for-weird's-sake, but there's something else going on here. There's a desolate, slightly sad weirdness to the whole business, and particularly the descriptions of the vendors. It feels oddly frustrated and dissipated, as if they're all stuck somewhere in a transitional purgatory of shit bootlegs and crappy merchandise, shifting stock endlessly, but to what end? This was mirrored quite nicely by the documentation, which seems like it has no idea what to make of this oddly gathering and brings up things that should be mentioned, like the 'History' addendum, only for there not really to be anything to say.

I kind of want Tales about these guys, which doesn't happen often. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr. Mangus is obviously bizzaro world, real life me.

I love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to DrBleep and ch00bakka for feedback and to ARD and Magnus for providing inspiration.

Image is CC by SA 3.0: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Jyv%C3%A4skyl%C3%A4_-_Sepp%C3%A4l%C3%A4_flea_market_2.jpg
